Home About Me Projects

Projects

tweetcost

A web app experiment to keep track of my personal finances by openly sharing it with the world. Tweetcost was a single-page-app that featured a large counter which would update every 2 minutes.

The idea was that I would tweet out the prices of my expenses as I traveled and meteor would query Twitter's api every 2 minutes. This would result in any new tweets with prices included and then it would parse and store the numerical value and add it to the total displayed on the front page.

USED

timyanchenwang.com

Revamping my personal site with github pages; using jekyll and, the very aptly named theme, "hyde".

The bulk of the work involved taking the "hyde" theme and pulling it apart while altering some of the ruby based jekyll components.

USED
